title: Pre-Award — Role of Principal Investigator
content:
This page explains who is eligible to serve as Principal Investigator (PI) or Project Director (PD) on a sponsored project at Morgan State University. For questions, contact the Office of Research Administration at ask.ora@morgan.edu or 443-885-4044.

Eligibility categories:
- Full-time tenure track faculty members at any academic rank automatically qualify as PI/PD.
- Other university appointees (directors, senior administrators, visiting faculty, retired faculty) may serve as PI/PD with dean approval. A formal Memorandum of Designation must be filed with ORA. Adjunct faculty serving as PI/PD may need a full-time co-PI/PD.
- Full-time regular staff with appropriate qualifications are eligible to serve as PI/PD.
- Individual fellowship applications: when a sponsor requires the Fellow to be the PI/PD, a Sponsor or Mentor meeting the criteria above will serve as the Project Director on MSU records and handle administrative and scientific oversight of the award.

key_facts.requires_dean_approval: directors, senior administrators, visiting faculty, retired faculty (Memorandum of Designation required)
key_facts.fellowship_exception: Sponsor/Mentor serves as PD when Fellow is required PI
